docs.title: AlphaPIL
docs.text: AlphaPIL is a powerful, template-based image generation library for Python. It simplifies complex image manipulations using a straightforward, scriptable syntax.

docs.title: Why AlphaPIL?
docs.text:
• 🎨 Syntax Driven - Create complex layouts with a simple, readable syntax.
  • ⚡ Performant - Built on top of PIL (Pillow) for fast image processing.
  • 🔧 Extensible - Easily add new functions and filters.
  • 🏆 Bot Ready - Perfect for Discord bots, automated reports, and social media cards.

docs.title: Quick Example
docs.text:
$createCanvas[400;200;#2C3E50]
$drawRect[10;10;380;180;#34495E;#1ABC9C]
$drawText[200;100;Hello AlphaPIL!;white;24;center]
$save[output.png]

docs.title: Community & Support
docs.text:
• GitHub Repository
  • Discord Server
  • Issue Tracker

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: Here are some complete examples of what you can build with AlphaPIL.

docs.title: Discord Rank Card
docs.text:
This example demonstrates how to use variables, shapes, and images to create a profile card.

# Set up variables
$setVar[name;{name}]
$setVar[xp;{xp}]
$setVar[level;{level}]
$setVar[rank;{rank}]

# Create canvas
$createCanvas[400;200;#2C3E50]

# Draw background and border
$drawRect[10;10;380;180;#34495E;#1ABC9C;none;2]

# Draw user info
$drawText[200;40;$toUpper[{name}];white;20;center]
$drawText[200;80;Level: {level};white;16;center]
$drawText[200;110;XP: {xp};white;16;center]
$drawText[200;140;Rank: {rank};gold;14;center]

# Draw avatar
$if[{avatar_url};$drawImage[20;20;{avatar_url};60;60;circle=true];]

# Save output
$save[rank_card.png]

docs.title: Gradient Text Banner
docs.text:
$createCanvas[600;150;#000000]
$drawTextGradient[ALPHAPIL;300;75;60;red;blue;bold;center]
$drawRect[10;10;580;130;none;white;none;1]
$save[banner.png]

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: Learn how to install and start using AlphaPIL in your projects.

docs.title: From PyPI (Recommended)
docs.text: pip install alphapil

docs.title: From Source (Development)
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/MeraLabs/AlphaPIL.git
cd AlphaPIL
pip install -e .

docs.title: Basic Usage
docs.text:
AlphaPIL uses a "Template Engine" to process image generation commands.

import asyncio
from alphapil import CanvasEngine

async def main():
    # Initialize the engine
    engine = CanvasEngine()

    # Define your template
    template = """
    $createCanvas[500;500;white]
    $drawCircle[250;250;100;red;black;none;5]
    $drawText[250;250;AlphaPIL;black;20;center]
    $save[circle.png]
    """

    # Render the template
    await engine.render(template)

asyncio.run(main())

docs.title: Using Variables
docs.text:
You can inject variables into your templates using the {var_name} syntax.

import asyncio
from alphapil import CanvasEngine

async def main():
    engine = CanvasEngine()

    data = {
        "name": "Alpha",
        "color": "#ff0000"
    }

    template = """
    $createCanvas[400;200;{color}]
    $drawText[200;100;Hello {name}!;white;30;center]
    $save[hello.png]
    """

    await engine.render(template, data)

asyncio.run(main())

docs.title: Python Integration
docs.text: AlphaPIL is designed to be easily integrated into any Python application, such as web servers (FastAPI/Flask) or Discord bots.

docs.title: Basic Usage
docs.text:
The CanvasEngine provides an asynchronous render_template method that returns the final image as bytes.

from alphapil import CanvasEngine
import asyncio

async def main():
    engine = CanvasEngine()

    template = """
    $createCanvas[400;200;white]
    $drawText[center;center;Hello {user};black;32]
    """

    # Render and get bytes
    image_bytes = await engine.render_template(template, {"user": "Alpha"})

    # Save to file
    with open("output.png", "wb") as f:
        f.write(image_bytes)

asyncio.run(main())

docs.title: Integration with FastAPI
docs.text:
You can serve generated images directly in a web response.

from fastapi import FastAPI, Response
from alphapil import CanvasEngine

app = FastAPI()
engine = CanvasEngine()

@app.get("/generate/{name}")
async def generate_image(name: str):
    template = """
    $createCanvas[500;200;#1a1a1a]
    $drawText[center;center;Welcome {name};white;40]
    """

    image_bytes = await engine.render_template(template, {"name": name})

    return Response(content=image_bytes, media_type="image/png")

docs.title: Integration with Discord.py
docs.text:
To use AlphaPIL in a Discord bot, convert the bytes into a discord.File using io.BytesIO.

import discord
from discord.ext import commands
from io import BytesIO
from alphapil import CanvasEngine

class ImageBot(commands.Cog):
    def __init__(self, bot):
        self.bot = bot
        self.engine = CanvasEngine()

    @commands.command()
    async def welcome(self, ctx):
        template = "$createCanvas[400;100;blue]$drawText[center;center;Welcome!;white;24]"

        # 1. Get bytes from engine
        image_bytes = await self.engine.render_template(template)

        # 2. Wrap in BytesIO and create discord.File
        file = discord.File(BytesIO(image_bytes), filename="welcome.png")

        # 3. Send to Discord
        await ctx.send(file=file)

async def setup(bot):
    await bot.add_cog(ImageBot(bot))

Tip

The render_template_file method is also available if you prefer keeping your AlphaPIL code in separate .txt files.

docs.title: Syntax Guide
docs.text: AlphaPIL uses a custom scripting syntax inspired by common bot commands and functional programming.

docs.title: Function Syntax
docs.text:
All functions start with a $ followed by the function name and arguments enclosed in [], separated by ;.

$functionName[arg1;arg2;arg3]

docs.title: Key Principles
docs.text:
1. Top-Down Execution: Templates are processed line by line.
  2. Case Sensitivity: Function names are case-sensitive (e.g., $drawRect vs $drawrect).
  3. Variables: Use {variable_name} to inject values into arguments.
  4. Colors: Supports hex codes (#ffffff), RGB/RGBA, and common color names (white, red, etc.).

docs.title: Function Nesting
docs.text: One of the most powerful features of AlphaPIL is nesting. Since functions return string values, you can use one function as an argument for another.

docs.title: How it works
docs.text:
The interpreter resolves functions from the inside-out.

Example: 

$drawRect[10;10;$math[100 + 50];$random[20;80];red]
In this case: 1. $math[100 + 50] is resolved to 150. 2. $random[20;80] is resolved to a random number (e.g., 42). 3. Finally, $drawRect[10;10;150;42;red] is executed.

docs.title: Composable Functions
docs.text: Functions that are commonly used inside others include: * Math & Logic: $math, $if, $random * Text Utilities: $toUpper, $toLower, $substring, $length * Color Utilities: $getHex

docs.title: Anchors (Pivot Points)
docs.text:
Most drawing functions support an anchor parameter. This defines which part of the object "sits" on the coordinates you provided.

Anchor Description lt Left-Top (Default for most) ct Center-Top rt Right-Top lm Left-Middle mm Middle-Middle (Center) rm Right-Middle lb Left-Bottom cb Center-Bottom rb Right-Bottom

Example: 

# Draws a 100x100 square centered exactly at 500,500
$drawRect[500;500;100;100;red;anchor=mm]

docs.title: Coordinate Grouping
docs.text:
Groups allow you to define a set of elements relative to a starting point. If you move the group, all elements inside move with it.

$startGroup[100;100]
  $drawRect[0;0;50;50;blue]  # Drawn at 100,100
  $drawCircle[25;25;10;white] # Drawn at 125,125
$endGroup

docs.title: Setting Variables
docs.text:
You can set internal template variables using $setVar.

$setVar[myColor;#ff5500]
$createCanvas[100;100;{myColor}]

docs.title: Dynamic Injection
docs.text:
When using the Python API, you can pass a dictionary of variables:

# Async context
await engine.render(template, data={"username": "John"})

In the template: 

$drawText[10;10;Hello {username}!;white;12]

docs.title: Mathematical Expressions
docs.text:
Use the $math function to perform calculations.

$setVar[centerX;$math[800 / 2]]
$setVar[centerY;$math[600 / 2]]
$drawCircle[{centerX};{centerY};50;blue]

docs.title: If Statements
docs.text:
The $if function allows for conditional rendering.

$if[{xp} > 1000;$drawText[10;10;Pro User;gold;16];$drawText[10;10;Newbie;white;16]]

Syntax: $if[condition;true_branch;false_branch]

docs.title: Core & System Commands
docs.text:
These functions manage the canvas, files, and core engine settings.

Function Description Syntax createCanvas Creates a new blank image. $createCanvas[width;height;color] save Saves the current canvas to a file. $save[filename] getBytes Returns the current canvas as bytes. $getBytes[format] setVar Sets a template-local variable. $setVar[name;value] setFont Sets the default font and size. $setFont[font_path;size] loadFont Loads a local or remote font (URL) with an alias. $loadFont[path_or_url;alias] setColor Sets the default fill color. $setColor[color] setStroke Sets the default stroke width and color. $setStroke[width;color]

docs.title: Creating and Saving
docs.text:
$createCanvas[800;600;#1a1a2e]
# ... drawing commands ...
$save[output.png]

docs.title: Managing Fonts
docs.text:
$loadFont[fonts/bold.ttf;mybold]
$drawText[10;10;Hello;white;24;mybold]

docs.title: Geometric Shapes
docs.text:
Functions for drawing basic and complex geometric shapes.

Function Syntax drawRect $drawRect[x;y;width;height;color;outline;fill;outline_width;radius;anchor] drawRoundedRect $drawRoundedRect[x;y;width;height;radius;color;outline;fill;outline_width] drawCircle $drawCircle[cx;cy;radius;color;outline;fill;outline_width] drawLine $drawLine[x1;y1;x2;y2;color;width] drawPolygon $drawPolygon[points_list;color;outline;fill;outline_width] drawTriangle $drawTriangle[x1;y1;x2;y2;x3;y3;color;outline;fill;outline_width] drawStar $drawStar[x;y;points;outer_radius;inner_radius;color;outline;fill;outline_width] drawArc $drawArc[x;y;w;h;start_angle;end_angle;color;width]

docs.title: Rectangles (including Rounded)
docs.text:
The $drawRect function now supports an optional radius parameter to create rounded rectangles.

# A red rectangle with white outline
$drawRect[50;50;200;100;color=red;outline=white;outline_width=2]

# A rounded rectangle with 20px radius
$drawRect[x=50;y=200;width=200;height=100;radius=20;color=blue]

docs.title: Complex Shapes
docs.text:
# A golden 5-point star
$drawStar[x=center;y=center;5;50;20;gold;white;none;2]

docs.title: Images & Filters
docs.text:
Functions for loading, manipulating, and filtering images.

Function Syntax drawImage $drawImage[x;y;path_or_url;w;h;opacity;radius;circle] useImageAsCanvas $useImageAsCanvas[path_or_url;height_var;width_var;fixed_width;fixed_height] imageFilter $imageFilter[filter_name] clearImageCache $clearImageCache[]

docs.title: Loading Avatars
docs.text:
You can load images directly from URLs: 

$drawImage[20;20;{avatar_url};60;60;circle=true]

docs.title: Background Images
docs.text: $useImageAsCanvas[assets/bg.png;h;w;800;600]

docs.title: Applying Filters
docs.text:
$imageFilter[blur]
$imageFilter[sharpen]

docs.title: Text & Typography
docs.text:
Functions for rendering and manipulating text.

Function Syntax drawText $drawText[x;y;text;color;size;font;anchor;...;max_width;truncate_width] text Alias for $drawText drawTextMid $drawTextMid[x1;y1;x2;y2;text;color;size;font;...] drawTextIn $drawTextIn[x;y;w;h;text;color;size;font;...] toUpper / toLower $toUpper[text] / $toLower[text] toTitle $toTitle[text] measureText $measureText[text;size;font] autoSizeText $autoSizeText[text;max_width;start_size;min_size;font] truncateText / wrapText $truncateText[text;width] / $wrapText[text;width]

docs.title: Advanced drawText Parameters
docs.text:
The $drawText function (aliased as $text) is highly versatile. It supports named parameters for advanced styling:

  • stroke_width: Width of the text outline.
  • stroke_fill: Color of the text outline.
  • gradient_colors: Comma-separated colors for a vertical gradient (e.g., red,blue).
  • max_width: Automatically wraps text to fit this width.
  • truncate_width: Automatically truncates text with ... to fit this width.
  • shadow_color & shadow_offset: Adds a drop shadow.
  • glow_color & glow_radius: Adds an outer glow effect.

docs.title: Example: Styled Text
docs.text:
$drawText[
    text=AlphaPIL;
    x=center;y=center;
    size=40;
    gradient_colors=gold,orange;
    stroke_width=2;stroke_fill=black;
    shadow_color=gray;shadow_offset=2,2
]

docs.title: Utils & Logic
docs.text:
Helper functions for calculations and conditional rendering.

Function Syntax math $math[expression] if $if[condition;true_val;false_val] random $random[min;max] length $length[text] replace $replace[text;old;new] substring $substring[text;start;end] split $split[text;separator] join $join[separator;item1;item2;...] getHex $getHex[color_name]

docs.title: Dynamic Positioning
docs.text:
$setVar[margin;20]
$setVar[textX;$math[{margin} + 10]]
$drawText[{textX};50;Indented Text;white;12]

docs.title: Random Elements
docs.text:
$setVar[randVal;$random[1;10]]
$if[{randVal} > 5;$drawCircle[100;100;50;green];$drawCircle[100;100;50;red]]

docs.title: createCanvas
docs.text: Creates a new blank image canvas to start drawing.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: $createCanvas[width;height;color]

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description Default width int The width of the canvas in pixels. Required height int The height of the canvas in pixels. Required color string Background color (Hex, RGB, or Name). #000000 (Black)

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
# Create a 1080p canvas with a dark blue background
$createCanvas[1920;1080;#000033]

Info

Every AlphaPIL script must start with $createCanvas or $loadImage to initialize the engine.

docs.title: loadFont
docs.text: Registers a font file from a local path or a remote URL with an alias.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: $loadFont[path_or_url;alias]

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description Default path_or_url string Local path to .ttf/.otf OR a direct URL to a font file. Required alias string A shorthand name to use instead of the full path/URL. Required

docs.title: Local Font
docs.text:
$loadFont[./assets/fonts/Bold.ttf;bold]
$drawText[50;50;Local Bold;white;24;bold]

docs.title: Remote Font (No download needed!)
docs.text:
$loadFont[https://github.com/google/fonts/raw/main/ofl/roboto/Roboto-Bold.ttf;roboto]
$drawText[50;150;Online Roboto;white;24;roboto]

docs.title: Where to find Font URLs?
docs.text:
To use remote fonts, you need a direct link to a .ttf or .otf file.

  1. Google Fonts (GitHub): The most reliable source. Visit the Google Fonts GitHub, navigate to a font, and copy the link to the "Raw" file.
  2. Discord: You can upload a font to a Discord channel, right-click the file, and select Copy Link.
  3. Open Source Projects: Many fonts like Inter or JetBrains Mono host their files on GitHub.

Warning

The URL must end in .ttf or .otf. Linking to a website like fonts.google.com will not work as those are preview pages, not the font files themselves.

docs.title: save
docs.text: Saves the current state of the canvas to an image file.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: $save[filename]

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description Default filename string The path and name of the file to save (e.g., output.png). Required

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
$createCanvas[400;400;white]
$drawCircle[200;200;100;red]
$save[final_image.png]

docs.title: setColor
docs.text: Sets the default primary color for drawing operations.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: $setColor[color]

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description Default color string Hex, RGB(A), or Name (e.g., #FF0000, red). Required

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
$setColor[blue]
$drawRect[0;0;100;100] # Draws a blue rectangle

docs.title: setFont
docs.text: Sets the default font and size for all subsequent text drawing commands.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: $setFont[font_path;size]

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description Default font_path string Path to a .ttf or .otf file, or a font alias. Required size int Default font size in pixels. 12

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
$loadFont[fonts/Inter.ttf;myFont]
$setFont[myFont;32]
$drawText[10;10;Default Style Text]

docs.title: setStroke
docs.text: Sets the default stroke (outline) properties.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: $setStroke[width;color]

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description Default width int Stroke thickness in pixels. Required color string Stroke color. black

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
$setStroke[5;red]
$drawRect[10;10;100;100;white;fill=none] # White box with 5px red border

docs.title: setVar
docs.text: Sets a template-local variable that can be used later in the script with {variable_name} syntax.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: $setVar[name;value]

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description Default name string The name of the variable (without {}). Required value string The value to assign to the variable. Required

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
$setVar[bgColor;#121212]
$createCanvas[500;500;{bgColor}]

docs.title: drawArc
docs.text: Draws an arc (part of an ellipse).

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: $drawArc[x;y;w;h;start_angle;end_angle;color;width]

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description Default x int/str Top-left X of the bounding box. Required y int/str Top-left Y of the bounding box. Required w int Width of the bounding box. Required h int Height of the bounding box. Required start_angle int Starting angle in degrees. Required end_angle int Ending angle in degrees. Required color string Arc color. black width int Arc thickness. 1

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
# Draw a semi-circle
$drawArc[100;100;200;200;0;180;red;5]

docs.title: drawCircle
docs.text: Draws a circle on the canvas.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: $drawCircle[cx;cy;radius;color;outline;fill;outline_width]

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description Default cx int/str X coordinate of the center. Required cy int/str Y coordinate of the center. Required radius int Radius of the circle. Required color string Primary color. black outline string Outline color. none fill string Fill color/style. solid outline_width int Stroke width. 1 anchor string Positioning pivot point. mm

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
# Red circle in the center
$drawCircle[center;center;50;red]

docs.title: drawLine
docs.text: Draws a straight line between two points.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: $drawLine[x1;y1;x2;y2;color;width]

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description Default x1 int/str Start X coordinate. Required y1 int/str Start Y coordinate. Required x2 int/str End X coordinate. Required y2 int/str End Y coordinate. Required color string Line color. black width int Line thickness. 1

docs.title: Example
docs.text: $drawLine[0;0;center;center;red;2]

docs.title: drawPolygon
docs.text: Draws a polygon with a custom list of points.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: $drawPolygon[points_list;color;outline;fill;outline_width]

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description Default points_list string Comma-separated points x1,y1,x2,y2,x3,y3.... Required color string Main color. black outline string Outline color. none fill string Fill style. solid outline_width int Stroke width. 1

docs.title: Example
docs.text: $drawPolygon[100,10,190,190,10,190;gold] # Draws a triangle

docs.title: drawRect
docs.text: Draws a rectangle on the canvas.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: $drawRect[x;y;width;height;color;outline;fill;outline_width]

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description Default x int/str X coordinate (or center). Required y int/str Y coordinate (or center). Required width int Width of the rectangle. Required height int Height of the rectangle. Required color string Primary color. Required outline string Outline color. none fill string Fill style (none or color). solid outline_width int Thickness of the outline. 1 radius int Corner radius for rounded rectangles. 0 anchor string Positioning pivot point. lt

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
# Draw a simple blue rectangle
$drawRect[50;50;200;100;blue]

# Draw a rectangle with a white outline and no fill
$drawRect[100;100;300;200;blue;white;none;5]

# Draw a rounded rectangle with radius 10
$drawRect[100;100;300;200;blue;white;none;5;10]

docs.title: drawRoundedRect
docs.text: Draws a rectangle with rounded corners.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: $drawRoundedRect[x;y;width;height;radius;color;outline;fill;outline_width]

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description Default x int/str X position. Required y int/str Y position. Required width int Width of the rectangle. Required height int Height of the rectangle. Required radius int Corner radius. 10 color string Main color. black outline string Outline color. none fill string Fill style. solid outline_width int Stroke width. 1

docs.title: Example
docs.text: $drawRoundedRect[50;50;200;100;20;blue]

docs.title: drawStar
docs.text: Draws a star shape.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: $drawStar[x;y;points;outer_radius;inner_radius;color;outline;fill;outline_width]

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description Default x int/str X position of the star center. Required y int/str Y position of the star center. Required points int Number of points on the star. Required outer_radius int Distance from center to outer points. Required inner_radius int Distance from center to inner points. Required color string Main color. black outline string Outline color. none fill string Fill style. solid outline_width int Stroke width. 1

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
# A classic 5-pointed star
$drawStar[center;center;5;100;40;gold]

docs.title: endGroup
docs.text: Ends the current coordinate group.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: $endGroup

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
$startGroup[100;100]
  $drawRect[0;0;50;50;blue]
$endGroup

docs.title: startGroup
docs.text: Starts a new coordinate group. All drawing commands inside the group use coordinates relative to the group's starting point.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: $startGroup[x;y]

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description Default x int/str X position for the group origin. Required y int/str Y position for the group origin. Required

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
$startGroup[100;100]
  # This circle will be drawn at 150, 150 on the main canvas
  $drawCircle[50;50;20;red]
$endGroup

Info

Groups can be nested. Offsets from parent groups are added together.

docs.title: drawImage
docs.text: Draws an image onto the canvas. Supports URLs, local files, and advanced styling.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: $drawImage[x;y;image_path;width;height;opacity;radius;circle]

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description Default x int/str X position. Required y int/str Y position. Required image_path string URL or local path to the image. Required width int Resize to width (maintains aspect if height omitted). None height int Resize to height. None opacity int Opacity percentage (0-100). 100 radius int Rounded corner radius. None circle bool Crop to a circle (true/false). false anchor string Positioning pivot point (e.g. mm, lt). lt (mm if circle=true)

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
# Draw a user's avatar as a circle centered at 200,200
$drawImage[200;200;https://example.com/avatar.png;100;100;circle=true]

docs.title: imageFilter
docs.text: Applies a visual filter to the entire current canvas.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: $imageFilter[filter_name]

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description filter_name string Name of the filter.

docs.title: Available Filters:
docs.text: blur, contour, detail, edge_enhance, emboss, find_edges, sharpen, smooth.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
$drawImage[0;0;photo.jpg]
$imageFilter[blur]

docs.title: createLayer
docs.text: Creates a new transparent layer for complex compositions.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: $createLayer[name]

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description name string Unique name for the layer.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
$createLayer[shadows]
$switchLayer[shadows]
# ... draw things on shadow layer ...
$switchLayer[main]

docs.title: mergeLayer
docs.text: Composites a layer onto the main canvas or another layer.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: $mergeLayer[name;x;y;opacity;target]

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description Default name string Source layer name. Required x, y int/str Position to paste the layer. 0, 0 opacity int Opacity percentage. 100 target string Target layer name (or main). main

docs.title: Example
docs.text: $mergeLayer[overlay;0;0;50;main]

docs.title: drawText
docs.text: Draws text onto the canvas with specified styling.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: $drawText[x;y;text;color;size;font_alias]

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description Default x int/str X coordinate (or center). Required y int/str Y coordinate (or center). Required text string The text to display. Required color string Text color. white size int Font size in pixels. 24 font_alias string Alias of a font loaded via $loadFont. default

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
$loadFont[fonts/Roboto-Bold.ttf;bold]
$drawText[center;center;Hello World!;white;48;bold]

docs.title: drawTextGradient
docs.text: Draws text with a vertical linear gradient.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: $drawTextGradient[text;x;y;size;color1;color2;font]

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description Default text string The text to draw. Required x int/str X position. Required y int/str Y position. Required size int Font size. Required color1 string Top color of the gradient. Required color2 string Bottom color of the gradient. Required font string Font path or alias. None

docs.title: Example
docs.text: $drawTextGradient[SUNSET;center;center;80;orange;purple]

docs.title: drawTextMid
docs.text: A powerful centering function that can center text within a box or between two points.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: $drawTextMid[x1;y1;x2;y2;text;color;size;font;...]

docs.title: Parameters (Partial List - Supports all Text Effects)
docs.text: Parameter Type Description Default x1, x2 int/str Centers horizontally between these two points. Optional y1, y2 int/str Centers vertically between these two points. Optional x, y, w, h int Centers within a box starting at (x,y) with width w and height h. Optional text string The text to draw. Required

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
# Center text in a 200x50 button box
$drawRect[10;10;200;50;blue]
$drawTextMid[x=10;y=10;w=200;h=50;text=Click Me;color=white;size=16]

docs.title: drawTextStroke
docs.text: Draws text with a dedicated outline (stroke).

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: $drawTextStroke[text;x;y;size;color;stroke_width;stroke_color;font]

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description Default text string The text to draw. Required x int/str X position. Required y int/str Y position. Required size int Font size. Required color string Inner text color. Required stroke_width int Thickness of the outline. Required stroke_color string Color of the outline. Required font string Font path or alias. None

docs.title: Example
docs.text: $drawTextStroke[OUTLINE;center;center;64;white;3;black]

docs.title: if
docs.text: Basic conditional logic.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: $if[condition;true_val;false_val]

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description condition string Expression to check (e.g., {val} == 1). true_val string Result if condition is true. false_val string Result if condition is false.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
$setVar[points;10]
$drawText[10;10;$if[{points} > 5;Winner;Loser]]

docs.title: Nesting
docs.text:
You can nest other functions inside the branches of an $if: 

$if[{level} > 5;$drawImage[0;0;badge.png];$drawText[0;0;No Badge;gray;12]]

docs.title: math
docs.text: Evaluates a mathematical expression and returns the result.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text: $math[expression]

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description expression string A valid math expression (e.g., 10 + 20 * 2).

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
$setVar[total;$math[50 * 2]]
$drawText[10;10;Result: {total}]

docs.title: Nesting
docs.text:
Because $math returns a number string, it can be used inside other functions: 

$drawRect[10;10;$math[100 + 50];50;red]

docs.title: random
docs.text: Generates a random integer within a specified range.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text:
$random[min;max]
# OR
$random[max] (uses 0 as minimum)

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Description Default min int Minimum value. 0 (if max omitted) max int Maximum value. Required

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
$setVar[posX;$random[0;800]]
$drawCircle[{posX};center;20;red]

docs.title: Nesting
docs.text:
This is very useful for adding "chaos" or variety to your templates: 

$drawRect[0;0;100;100;rgb($random[255],$random[255],$random[255])]
